issues: The draft says Humboldt scored once in overtime ('the overtime winner'), but the OCR clearly states the Broncos scored TWICE in overtime to reach 5-3 — the draft's score of 5-3 is correct but the overtime description is understated/misleading.
The draft omits that Brad Bergen and Bill McDougall scored the other Humboldt goals — not a critical omission but the long description should be accurate.
The draft says 'best-of-seven' but the OCR only says '-of-seven' (OCR noise obscuring the prefix). 'Best-of-seven' is a reasonable assumption but not confirmed by OCR.
Byline is listed as 'Canadian Press' which is correct per OCR — this is fine.
The draft says 'Curtis Chamberlain scored three goals including the overtime winner' — this is accurate per OCR, no issue there.
'Bryan Moller' is spelled 'Bryon Moller' on the roster but OCR says 'Bryan Moller' — draft matches OCR.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Richmond Sockeyes fall 5-3 to Humboldt Broncos, who scored twice in overtime, in Game 1 of the Centennial Cup Western Canada junior hockey final.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: The Richmond Sockeyes lost 5-3 to the Humboldt Broncos on April 21, 1987, in Game 1 of the Centennial Cup Western Canada junior hockey final after Humboldt scored twice in overtime. Curtis Chamberlain led the Saskatchewan champions with three goals including the overtime winner, while Brad Bergen and Bill McDougall added the others. Richmond had led 2-1 after two periods and the teams were tied 3-3 at the end of regulation, with Sockeyes goals from Bryan Moller, Matt Hervey, and Jason Phillips.
review.notes: The key factual error is that Humboldt scored TWICE in overtime (not once), per the OCR. The draft's phrasing implies only a single overtime goal (the winner). Also note the OCR prefix before "-of-seven" is garbled — "best-of-seven" is assumed but not confirmed. Headline and byline are accurately captured from the OCR.
